sections.topic: Language: Skill, Tool, Form, and Function

pre_reading.question: What is language?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Language is a skill, a tool for communication, a form of verbal and non-verbal expression, and a means to perform social functions.

pre_reading.question: What do we use it for?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: We use it to communicate with others, share information, express our feelings, ask questions, and perform daily social tasks.

pre_reading.question: What does language do for us?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Language helps us connect with people, reflects and preserves our culture, passes traditions to the next generation, and defines our social identity.

pre_reading.question: What would our life be like without language?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Without language, our life would be incredibly difficult because we would not be able to share complex ideas, express thoughts clearly, or build organized societies.

paragraphs.english_text: The question, 'What is language?' can have several different answers, and all of them may be acceptable. For our present purpose, however, let us look at language as: skill, tool, form, and function.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဘာသာစကားဆိုတာ ဘာလဲဆိုတဲ့ မေးခွန်းအတွက် လက်ခံနိုင်တဲ့ အဖြေတွေ အများကြီးရှိပါတယ်။ ဒါပေမယ့် ဒီသင်ခန်းစာမှာတော့ ဘာသာစကားကို ကျွမ်းကျင်မှု (skill)၊ ကိရိယာ (tool)၊ ပုံသဏ္ဌာန် (form) နဲ့ လုပ်ဆောင်ချက် (function) ဆိုပြီး ရှုထောင့်လေးခုကနေ လေ့လာသွားပါမယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: First, language is a set of skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing. They are often known as the four language skills. Of these four skills, the listening skill is the first to develop. It develops during the first eighteen months of babyhood. The speaking skill develops at about the age of one and a half years or so. By about four or five years of age, the child is able to use his or her mother tongue quite efficiently. It is only when the child goes to school that he or she learns to read and write.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ပထမအချက်အနေနဲ့ ဘာသာစကားဟာ နားထောင်ခြင်း၊ ပြောဆိုခြင်း၊ ဖတ်ရှုခြင်းနဲ့ ရေးသားခြင်းဆိုတဲ့ 'ဘာသာစကား စွမ်းရည်လေးခု' ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ကလေးတစ်ယောက်မှာ ပထမဆုံး စတင်ဖွံ့ဖြိုးတာက နားထောင်မှုစွမ်းရည် (အသက် ၁၈ လအတွင်း) ဖြစ်ပြီး၊ အသက် တစ်နှစ်ခွဲလောက်မှာ စကားစပြောလာပါတယ်။ အသက် ၄ နှစ် သို့မဟုတ် ၅ နှစ်မှာ မိခင်ဘာသာစကားကို ကျွမ်းကျင်စွာ သုံးနိုင်လာပြီး၊ ကျောင်းတက်တဲ့အခါမှသာ စာဖတ်ခြင်းနဲ့ စာရေးခြင်းကို သင်ယူရတာ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Here, we should note that listening and speaking work together as a pair of skills, while reading and writing work together as another pair. We should note further that when we are listening or reading, we are not producing language; we are receiving language. That is why these two skills are often known as receptive skills. When we are speaking or writing, however, we are producing language. Thus, speaking and writing are often known as productive skills. (See the diagram below.)
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: မှတ်သားရမှာက နားထောင်ခြင်းနဲ့ ပြောဆိုခြင်းက တွဲလျက် စွမ်းရည်တစ်ခုဖြစ်ပြီး၊ ဖတ်ခြင်းနဲ့ ရေးခြင်းက အခြားအတွဲတစ်ခု ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ နားထောင်တဲ့အခါ ဒါမှမဟုတ် စာဖတ်တဲ့အခါမှာ ဘာသာစကားကို လက်ခံရယူတာ ဖြစ်လို့ လက်ခံစွမ်းရည် (Receptive Skills) လို့ ခေါ်ပါတယ်။ စကားပြောတဲ့အခါနဲ့ စာရေးတဲ့အခါမှာတော့ ဘာသာစကားကို ထုတ်လုပ်သုံးစွဲတာ ဖြစ်လို့ ထုတ်လုပ်မှုစွမ်းရည် (Productive Skills) လို့ ခေါ်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Second, language is a tool for communication. This means that we use language to speak to someone, or to write to someone. To speak, we use vocal symbols; to write, we use written symbols. What is interesting here is that when we speak, we use gestures such as the movement of our arms, hands, heads, and also facial expressions to help the listener understand us better. When we write, however, we use graphics such as pictures, photographs, charts, tables, etc. to help the reader understand us better. Thus, we may say that speaking and gestures go hand in hand, just as writing and graphics do.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဒုတိယအချက်ကတော့ ဘာသာစကားဟာ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးအတွက် ကိရိယာတစ်ခု ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ စကားပြောဖို့ အသံသင်္ကေတ (vocal symbols) ကို သုံးပြီး၊ စာရေးဖို့ စာသားသင်္ကေတ (written symbols) ကို သုံးပါတယ်။ စကားပြောတဲ့အခါ နားထောင်သူ ပိုနားလည်အောင် မျက်နှာအမူအရာ၊ လက်ဟန်ခြေဟန် (gestures) တွေကို တွဲသုံးလေ့ရှိပြီး၊ စာရေးတဲ့အခါမှာတော့ ရုပ်ပုံ၊ ဇယား၊ မြေပုံ (graphics) တွေကို တွဲသုံးပါတယ်။ ဒါကြောင့် စကားပြောခြင်းနဲ့ လက်ဟန်ခြေဟန်၊ စာရေးခြင်းနဲ့ ဂရပ်ဖစ်ပုံဖော်မှုတွေဟာ ဒွန်တွဲနေပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Third, communication may be in one of two forms: verbal communication, and non-verbal communication. In verbal communication, we use words and sentences. They may be spoken or written. In non-verbal communication, on the other hand, we use gestures and graphics. (See the diagram below.)
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: တတိယအချက်အနေနဲ့ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးမှာ ပုံသဏ္ဌာန်နှစ်မျိုး ရှိပါတယ်။ ၎င်းတို့မှာ နှုတ်မှုရေးရာ ဆက်သွယ်ခြင်း (Verbal) နဲ့ နှုတ်မဲ့ဆက်သွယ်ခြင်း (Non-verbal) တို့ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ Verbal မှာ စကားလုံးတွေနဲ့ ဝါကျတွေကို (အပြော သို့မဟုတ် အရေး) သုံးပြီး၊ Non-verbal မှာတော့ စကားလုံးမပါဘဲ လက်ဟန်ခြေဟန်နဲ့ ဂရပ်ဖစ်ပုံတွေကို သုံးပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Fourth, we use language to help us perform many social functions, which are very much part of our day-to-day life. This means that we use language: to greet or to bid farewell, to pass on information, to ask someone a question or two, to request, to ask for permission, to instruct someone what to do or how to do something, to direct someone how to get to a certain place, to thank or express gratitude, to respond to words of thanks and gratitude, to apologize or express regret, to agree or disagree, and to perform many, many other functions.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: စတုတ္ထအချက်ကတော့ ဘာသာစကားကို နေ့စဉ်လူမှုဘဝ လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်တွေ (Social functions) မှာ သုံးတာ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဥပမာ - နှုတ်ဆက်ခြင်း၊ သတင်းအချက်အလက်ပေးခြင်း၊ မေးခွန်းမေးခြင်း၊ အကူအညီတောင်းခြင်း၊ ခွင့်ပြုချက်တောင်းခြင်း၊ ညွှန်ကြားခြင်း၊ လမ်းညွှန်ခြင်း၊ ကျေးဇူးတင်ခြင်း၊ တောင်းပန်ခြင်းနဲ့ သဘောတူ/မတူ ငြင်းခုံခြင်း စတဲ့ နေရာစုံမှာ သုံးစွဲပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: In addition, language reflects culture, preserves culture, passes on culture from one generation to the next, preserves nationality, and also identifies social class.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဒါ့အပြင် ဘာသာစကားဟာ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုကို ထင်ဟပ်စေတယ်၊ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုကို ထိန်းသိမ်းစောင့်ရှောက်ပြီး မျိုးဆက်တစ်ခုကနေ တစ်ခုကို လက်ဆင့်ကမ်းပေးတယ်၊ အမျိုးသားရေးလက္ခဏာကို တည်တံ့စေသလို လူမှုအတန်းအစားကိုလည်း ခွဲခြားသိရှိနိုင်စေပါတယ်။

part_A.instructions: Complete each sentence below with a word or phrase from the reading passage.

exercises.text: The four language skills are: ___________.
The first language skill to develop is ___________.
A baby begins to speak at the age of ___________.
exercises.answer: about one and a half years or so

exercises.text: Listening and ___________ work together as a pair of skills.
The other pair of skills includes ___________.
Speaking and writing are ___________ skills.
Language is also known as a ___________ for communication.
The two forms of communication are ___________ and ___________.
We use ___________ when we speak to help the listener understand better.
We use ___________ when we write to help the reader understand better.

part_B.instructions: Give a short answer to each question below.

exercises.question: When does a child begin to read and write?
exercises.answer: Only when the child goes to school.

exercises.question: What are the productive skills of language?
What are the receptive skills of language?
Why do we use gestures when we speak?
exercises.answer: To help the listener understand us better.

exercises.question: How do we help the reader understand what we write better?
exercises.answer: By using graphics such as pictures, photographs, charts, and tables.

exercises.question: What are the two forms of communication?
Do you want to learn any other foreign language apart from English? Why?
exercises.answer: Yes, I want to learn Japanese because I want to study abroad and explore Japanese culture.

exercises.question: Which language skill is the most difficult for you to learn? Why?
exercises.answer: Writing is the most difficult skill for me because it requires a lot of practice and attention to grammar and spelling.

part_C.instructions: Write the function of each utterance within the brackets given.

exercises.utterance: May I use the phone, please?
It's very kind of you to help me.
Could you please scan the photo for me?
Go along this street and turn right.
exercises.function: directing someone how to get to a certain place
responding to words of thanks and gratitude
asking someone a question or two

exercises.utterance: I don't quite agree with what you said.
Make three copies of this article.

part_A.instructions: Complete the table. Australia and China are done for you as examples.

part_B.instructions: Rewrite each sentence below, filling the blanks with appropriate words from the table in Exercise A.

exercises.text: Because I speak ___________ fluently, I did not have much trouble in France.
Those tourists are from Italy and they speak only ___________ and do not understand a word of English.
I wish to visit Vietnam but I do not speak a word of ___________.
Since you speak ___________ quite well, you should have no problem studying in the USA.
I plan to work in Korea, so I'm learning ___________ at the Yangon University of Foreign Languages.
A kimono is a traditional ___________ garment.
William Shakespeare is a very widely known ___________ playwright and poet.
I have never been to Laos and I have never tried ___________ food.
He grew up in Germany and he speaks ___________ fluently.
I like ___________ food though I don't know how to use the chopstick.

part_A.instructions: Spot the nouns in apposition in the following sentences and underline them.

exercises.text: There are many pagodas in Bagan, an ancient capital of Myanmar.
The ostrich, a kind of flightless bird, is found only in Africa.
My son, a musician, earns little and lives with me.
Loch Ness, a large mountain lake, is in Scotland.
Mt. Everest, the highest peak in the world, is in Nepal.
apposition_phrases: the highest peak in the world

exercises.text: I'd love to climb Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Tanzania.
Brussels sprout, a green vegetable like a tiny cabbage, is quite delicious to eat.
apposition_phrases: a green vegetable like a tiny cabbage

exercises.text: The Nile, the longest river in the world, is in northeastern Africa.
apposition_phrases: the longest river in the world

exercises.text: Judo, a Japanese martial art, originated from jujitsu, a samurai unarmed fighting technique.
